reasoning: The review of HHS guidance on water fluoridation was announced on April 10, 2025. While HHS Secretary RFK Jr. has expressed strong opposition to fluoridation, the decision to undertake a formal review rather than immediately pulling the CDC recommendation on Day 1 suggests the administration is following standard bureaucratic procedures for updating scientific guidance. Formal health and safety reviews—even fast-tracked ones—typically take many months due to the need for committee evaluations, evidence assessments, and internal government vetting. However, because this is only an agency recommendation (not a formal regulation requiring standard Administrative Procedure Act notice-and-comment periods), the HHS secretary has the authority to have the webpage pulled or archived at any time before July 1. Given the short 6-week window between the knowledge cutoff (May 20) and the resolution date (July 1), the bureaucratic inertia favors the page remaining online through the deadline as the review continues, but the risk of an abrupt takedown or interim suspension keeps the probability somewhat low.
batch_queries: Will the CDC still recommend 0.7 mg/L fluoride as the optimal level in community water supplies on July 1, 2025?
